name: David Cameron
role: Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
bio: British politician who served as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 2010 to 2016 and Leader of the Conservative Party.
_evidence: As Prime Minister, he called the referendum and led the official 'Remain' campaign, staking his political career on the outcome.

name: Boris Johnson
role: Member of Parliament / Former Mayor of London
bio: A prominent British politician who served as Mayor of London and later became a central figure in the Vote Leave campaign.
_evidence: He was the most prominent high-profile figure to join the 'Leave' campaign, significantly shifting public opinion toward Brexit.

name: Michael Gove
bio: A senior British Conservative politician who served in various cabinet roles, including Secretary of State for Justice during the referendum.
_evidence: He was a leading cabinet minister who broke with the Prime Minister to co-chair the Vote Leave campaign.

name: Nigel Farage
role: Leader of the UK Independence Party (UKIP)
bio: British politician and broadcaster who served as the leader of the UK Independence Party and was a key campaigner for Brexit.
_evidence: He was the primary driver of the anti-EU sentiment in British politics for years, forcing the referendum to happen.

name: Jeremy Corbyn
bio: British politician who served as Leader of the Labour Party and Leader of the Opposition from 2015 to 2020.
_evidence: As Leader of the Opposition, he officially supported 'Remain' but was criticized for a perceived lack of enthusiasm in his campaigning.
